title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXXXIX. Love And Matrimony.
content:
Margaret wrote a letter,
                            Seal'd it with her finger,
                            Threw it in the dam
                            For the dusty miller.
                            Dusty was his coat,
                            Dusty was the siller,
                            Dusty was the kiss
                            I'd from the dusty miller.
                            If I had my pockets
                            Full of gold and siller,
                            I would give it all
                            To my dusty miller.

        Chorus. O the little, little,
                            Rusty, dusty, miller.
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CCCCLXXXIX. Love And Matrimony."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
